response.justification: While Bogdan has strong experience in high-performance distributed systems and event-driven architectures, his background is almost entirely in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not C++. His only C++ exposure was a short 6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project. The role requires deep C++ and STL expertise as its foundational requirement, and the candidate shows no experience in industrial automation, IEC 62443 security standards, industrial communication protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or any embedded/on-premises industrial platform context.
strengths: Proven experience building high-performance, low-latency distributed systems handling millions of requests per second (Sociomantic)
Strong event-driven and message-broker architecture background via GISCollective and Optilyz
Solid software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, agile, and mentoring — aligning with the behavioral competencies
concerns: Negligible C++ experience — only ~6 months at National Instruments and a thesis project; the entire career is built on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang
Zero exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443 security standards, or industrial protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus TCP/IP, DNP 3.0, IEC 61850)
No evidence of secure development lifecycle (SDL) practices, application security risk work, or any cybersecurity domain knowledge relevant to this role
